# open-compute

> Model-agnostic computer-use core: one agent loop drives any reasoning model
> behind a single interface -- Anthropic Claude and OpenAI CUA are equally-ranked
> API backends, mock is the offline default. A keyless path also exists today via
> Mode A (the host model reasons itself, inline or in a self-spawned subagent for
> context economy). With normalized coordinates, a canonical action schema, a
> hybrid perception layer, and a central safety gate.

## Last-checked: 2026-06-26

## What it does

open-compute is a small, dependency-light Python core for building computer-use
agents (GUI/desktop/browser automation driven by an LLM). It realizes the
perception -> model-tool-call -> action -> feedback loop and keeps the model
backend swappable. Importing the package never requires a vendor SDK; the
`anthropic` and `openai` packages are lazy, optional extras. The default wiring
(mock backend + mock executor) runs fully offline, which is what the test suite
uses.

## Audience

- Developers building computer-use / GUI / browser agents who want to swap the
  reasoning model (Claude, OpenAI, mock) without rewriting the loop, or run
  keyless via Mode A (host model reasons itself; inline or self-spawned subagent).
- Researchers comparing pixel-vision vs accessibility/Set-of-Marks perception.
- Anyone who needs a backend-neutral action schema with one place for DPI /
  resolution normalization and one place for a confirm-before-risky safety gate.

## Architecture

```
            AGENT LOOP / ORCHESTRATOR  (loop.py)
            goal -> perceive -> backend -> safety -> execute -> re-perceive
              |              |               |              |
        Perception      Backend        SafetyPolicy     Executor (driver)
     (screenshot +    (ComputerBackend  (confirm /     (Mock / Browser / OS)
      set-of-marks /   protocol:         allow_all /
      accessibility)   Claude/OpenAI/    read_only)
                       Mock)
                          |
              Canonical Action schema (actions.py)
              normalized 0..1 coords -> denormalize per backend
```

## Core modules

- `actions.py` -- canonical `Action` / `ActionType`; pure `to_claude` /
  `to_openai` mappers. (fully implemented + tested)
- `coordinates.py` -- normalize / denormalize / rescale. (fully implemented + tested)
- `safety.py` -- `SafetyPolicy` permission gate. (fully implemented + tested)
- `config.py` -- `Config` dataclass + JSON loader. (fully implemented)
- `loop.py` -- `AgentLoop` orchestrator. (fully implemented + tested via mocks)
- `backends/` -- `ComputerBackend` protocol, `get_backend` factory, `MockBackend`
  (fully implemented), `ClaudeComputerBackend` (implemented; lazy `anthropic`),
  `OpenAIComputerBackend` (implemented; lazy `openai`; model name [UNSICHER]).
- `drivers/` -- `Executor`/`BrowserDriver`/`OSDriver` protocols (interfaces only)
  and `MockExecutor` (fully implemented).
- `perception.py` -- `ScreenshotPerception` (implemented); Set-of-Marks /
  accessibility / DOM providers (STUBS).
- `.github/workflows/tests.yml` -- mock-only pytest and compile check on
  Python 3.10, 3.11, and 3.12.

## Backend matrix

- mock   -- no SDK, offline, deterministic. Default.
- claude -- Anthropic Messages API + `computer` tool `computer_20251124`,
            beta header `computer-use-2025-11-24`, global pixel coordinates,
            host executes. Needs `pip install open-compute[claude]`.
- openai -- OpenAI computer-use; model `computer-use-preview` (configurable,
            [UNSICHER]). Needs `pip install open-compute[openai]`.
- (keyless) -- NOT a backend row. Mode A: the host model reasons itself, inline
            or in a self-spawned subagent (context economy, same model). Usage
            pattern, see ARCHITECTURE.md "Host-Modell-Kontext: Inline (a) vs.
            Selbst-Subagent (b)".
- local (foreign reasoner) -- SEPARATE, LOW-PRIORITY, OPTIONAL idea (not (b),
            not scheduled): a different model as reasoner (Ollama, or agy /
            codex / kimi). Would be a real new backend. See ARCHITECTURE.md.

## Install

```
pip install open-compute              # core only, zero runtime deps
pip install open-compute[claude]      # + anthropic SDK
pip install open-compute[openai]      # + openai SDK
```

## Quick start

```python
from open_compute import AgentLoop, Config
loop = AgentLoop(Config(backend="mock"))
print(loop.run("open settings and enable dark mode").done)
```

## Safety

Every action passes a central policy gate before execution. Default mode is
`confirm` (risky actions require confirmation). Computer-use is powerful; run
real backends in an isolated VM/container, keep a human in the loop, and treat
on-screen content as untrusted (prompt-injection risk).
